feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Apps
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Apps Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Build Action: Error fetching BTP subaccount destinations for subscriber tenant context.
entry.content:
I'm trying to test an Action in SAP Build. However, when trying to select a Destination, I get the the following error:
 
Error fetching BTP subaccount destinations for subscriber tenant context.
This error could happen for the following reasons:
Correlation ID: xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
 
This is happening for all Actions.

entry.title: Error while Enable Mobile services in SAP BTP Build Apps Trail account
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am using SAP BTP Build Apps trail account and created application.

As enable mobile service is mandatory before deploy android application I have tried to enable mobile service and getting below error.

I have contacted SAP Trail support team and they suggested to check Mobile service dashboard for error logs but I don't see any error logs. As SAP Trail support team suggested I am creating this question in forum. Please help with solution. Thanks in advance

entry.title: Difference in behavior of action call when called in debug mode vs unattended mode?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing the following issue on one particular action call.

Issue details:

1. Testing the action call directly on the action project works and provides the expected result.

2. Added the same action project as a dependency in an automation. Calling the action call via action groups. When executing this action group call in debug mode, provides the expected result.

3. Only when running the automation in unattended mode, the action call provides an unexpected result.

Already checked the following:

1. oData call on gateway server is working correctly.

2. Latest version is used for both manual and automated testing.

3. Same destination is used in debug and unattended mode.

4. Same data tested in both cases.

I am unsure of the direction I should next take to identify the issue.

Any direction or pointers would be much appreciated.

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: Something Went wrong
entry.content: One of my pages does this, any idea why this happens, but all other pages function well.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - Set App Variable
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have an app variable called 'Purchase Order Item' that is a list of objects. I have 2 pages ie. Item Details and Item Overview. Item Details page takes user inputs for an item and Item Overview page displays all the items as a list.

I have created a button 'Add Item' on the Item Details page so the item can be added to the Item Overview Page. In the logic canvas of the button, I have used 'Set App Variable' function to update my item list on Overview Page. This function is not appending to the existing items but rather replacing it. For example, if there is item 1 on Overview page and I try to append item 2 to it from Details page, it is replacing item 1 with item 2 rather than appending. Could you please suggest any ideas to resolve this?

Thank you. 

Kind Regards,

Praneetha

#SAPBuildApps

entry.title: how to connect ABAP cloud system with SAP build apps?
entry.content:
Can some one please guide us how to connect an ABAP cloud system/or S/4 hana cloud which is created as an instance in Free Tier BTP to a sap build apps (also free tier) ??

Even after entering all details we get errors of no authorization 

If want to try out real time scenarios like with data from SAP backend or sap abap cloud systems what are the steps to follow ? 

As even cloud connector needs a installation and need admin privileges ,isnt there a cloud way to connect to SAP backend system to BTP without installing new software on machine?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - Set App Variable
entry.content:
When you click on the "Set App Variable" logic function, on the right panel, you would see "Variable name" and "Assigned Value". In the "Assigned value", you have to choose "Formula" option. A formula editor pop up shows up. You can use the WITH_ITEM formula for this case since you want to append an item to a list. The item should be of the same data type as the list elements.

For eg: Lets say you have a list1(list of objects) which contains the items displayed on the overview page and you want to add item2 to list1 on the click of a button, the formula you would use in the formula editor would be:

WITH_ITEM(appVars.list1, item2) -> change the list1, item2 according to your variable names

The Formula editor shows how WITH_ITEM should be used, on the right side with examples.

entry.title: Fetching the Count from OData service response
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I am building an application using SAP Build Apps, integrating it with SAP SuccessFactors Platform with OData services.

On a view, I turned on the Include total count feature from the Paging option, found on the Get Record Collection flow logic.

With this, I get the data from SAP SuccessFactor, in below format:
{
"d": {
"results": [ ... ],
"__count": "381"
}
}

Can anyone please help me understand, how can I use/bind the value coming in the field - __count , so that I can display the count of the records?

Regards,

Chandan

entry.title: Build a Simple CRUD Application with SAP Build on SAP BTP Cockpit
entry.content:
Introduction
In today’s application landscape, CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) functionalities serve as the backbone of most business solutions. SAP Build Apps, available via SAP BTP Cockpit, allows developers and business users alike to rapidly design and deploy full-stack applications using a no-code/low-code approach. This blog guides you through the steps to develop a basic CRUD app and manage it within SAP BTP Cockpit.

Prerequisites
Before proceeding, make sure you have the following in place:
An active SAP BTP account (Free Tier or Enterprise)
SAP Build Apps service enabled in your subaccount
Access to SAP BTP Cockpit
Familiarity with basic data structures (Entities and Fields)

Step 1: Prepare Environment in SAP BTP Cockpit
Log in to the SAP BTP Cockpit.
Create a new subaccount and select your preferred region.
Navigate to the Service Marketplace and enable SAP Build Apps.
Assign the necessary roles such as SAP Build Apps Admin and Developer.
(Optional) Set up destinations if you plan to connect external services or backends.

Step 2: Start Your Application in SAP Build Apps
Open SAP Build Apps from the BTP Cockpit.
Click on Create New App.
Provide a name (e.g., “Task Manager”) and choose a layout.
Create a Data Resource, either using local storage or a connected data source.
Add a Data Entity (e.g., "Tasks") with fields like id, title, description, and status.

Step 3: Create the UI for CRUD Features
Create:
Add a form with input fields for each task attribute.
Use a "Create" button with logic to insert new records into the "Tasks" entity.
Read:
Design a container with a Repeat with binding to list all tasks.
Map data fields to their respective UI components.
Update:
Design a Detail View for editing selected task data.
Pre-populate form fields and connect an "Update" button with update logic.
Delete:
Add a Delete button or icon beside each task item.
Connect it to Delete Record logic and include a confirmation dialog.

Step 4: (Optional) Integrate with External Backend
If connecting to CAPM or external APIs, define a Destination in the SAP BTP Cockpit.
Set up proper authentication and headers.
Use REST API Direct Integration in SAP Build Apps to map backend services to CRUD actions.

Step 5: Preview and Test Your Application
Use the Preview App option within SAP Build Apps.
Test all features — create, view, edit, and delete tasks.
Utilize alerts and console logging for debugging and validation.

Step 6: Deploy and Monitor Using SAP BTP Cockpit
Publish your application for web or mobile use.
Return to the BTP Cockpit to:
Monitor usage and logs
Manage authorizations and roles
Verify destination and connectivity settings

Conclusion
Congratulations! You’ve successfully created a working CRUD application using SAP Build Apps within SAP BTP Cockpit. This streamlined process enhances productivity, minimizes development time, and provides seamless SAP ecosystem integration.

entry.title: Blog Post Still 'Awaiting Review' After 24 Hours – Request for Update
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community Team,

I submitted a blog post titled "Build and Deploy a Full-Stack SAP CAPM App with Fiori UI on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry" more than 24 hours ago. It's still showing as “Awaiting Review”, and the URL is not publicly accessible yet.

Could you please let me know the estimated time for moderation or if any action is required from my side?

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best regards,
Madhan

entry.title: SAP Build Apps/Process Automation: "Workflow Definition Not Found" Post Shared Environment Redeploy
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm encountering a frustrating issue with SAP Build Process Automation and its integration with SAP Build Apps, and I'm hoping someone here might have some insights.

I initially deployed my process to the Public Environment and was able to successfully trigger it from an SAP Build App without any problems. However, due to the need to enable notifications, I had to switch to a Shared Environment. After redeploying the same process to this new shared environment, the integration with SAP Build Apps stopped working.

I'm now consistently receiving the following error message when trying to trigger the workflow from my SAP Build App:

 

{"error":{"code":"bpm.workflowruntime.rest.definition.not.found","message":"Workflow definition not found","logId":"xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"}}

This clearly indicates that the SAP Build App can no longer locate the workflow definition in the new environment.

Here's what I've already tried to resolve the issue, without success:

  1. Deployed the process into a brand new project in the Shared Environment.
  2. Verified the deployment status in the Library within SAP Build Process Automation to ensure the process is indeed deployed and active in the Shared Environment.
  3. Reconfigured the integration within the SAP Build App by updating the process and action references to point to the newly deployed process in the Shared Environment.
  4. Created a completely new action within the SAP Build App, linking it to the redeployed process.

Despite these steps, the "Workflow definition not found" error persists. It seems like the SAP Build App is still looking for the old definition in the Public Environment, or perhaps there's a caching issue, or a deeper configuration problem with Shared Environments that I'm overlooking.

Has anyone encountered a similar problem when migrating processes from a Public to a Shared Environment, or when dealing with "Workflow definition not found" errors after a redeployment? Any suggestions or troubleshooting steps would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Embedded map view doesn't show
entry.content:
I am creating an App with SAP Build Apps which requires the Embedded map view. A week ago I implemented that map and it worked fine. But two days ago it just stopped showing up. It doesn't show up on Desktop or Mobile. I tried multiple different browsers. I also created a new Project with just the map. Which also didn't work.

Was there an Update on Wendnesday? Before that day it all worked fine.

entry.title: SAP UI5 components in SAP Build apps
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working and playing around in SAP Build apps(Mobile and Web application). I wondered that is there any way to use the SAP UI5 components in  SAP Build apps?.

Looking forward to hear some interesting things about it.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps: how do you mitigate the CORS issue when the app accesses another SAP BTP service?
entry.content:
I wonder what is the best method to solve the CORS issue specifically in the SAP Build Apps web app?

For example: I built an app which uses SAP AI Core. In the preview it works like charm, but when deployed, it throws the HTTP 403 error, which for sure is CORS-related.

What is the best method to solve this issue? Some properties in the BTP destination, defined in the Build Apps subaccount and leading to the SAP AI Core? Or any other method? Any ideas?

entry.title: Issues with page variable not being able to store values from data variable
entry.content:
Hi

URGENT HELP NEEDED

Any one facing issues with page variable not being able to store values from repeated.current formula; the data is being fetched from firestore database?

Note: Android preview works fine.

@timokapanen  if you could please check?

Regards

Abdul Latif

entry.title: APPGYVER Embedded Map View not rendering
entry.content:
• Problem: The built-in Map component no longer renders on any platform.

  • Tested On:

    • Existing app

    • New blank app (default map settings)

    • AppGyver Web Preview

    • AppGyver Android Preview

    • Generated APK (Android)

  • Symptoms:

    • Blank space where map should render

    • No console errors or loading indicators

  • Worked Before: Yes, up until aproximally 15th of June.

  • Urgency: Critical, app feature depends on built-in map.

Please advise if this is a known issue or when a fix is expected.

Thanks!

entry.title: build error on Build apps runtime 4.15.24
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I'm getting the error below while trying to build my appgyver web app.
The error is occurring even in versions previously build perfectly in the past.

Hope the team find a solution. Thanks! 

Build Log
AB version: 3.30.1
COMMAND: 

USER_METERING_ENABLED=true SHOW_SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION=false SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION_DISMISSIBLE=true NEXT_SUPPORT_RELATIVE_PATHS=true NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=2000 --no-experimental-global-navigator  npxrnv export -p web -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --packageManager yarn 

FAILED with ERROR: 

warn: You have integration @sba-runtime/integration-builder defined, but it wasn't found in package.json. ERR: Error: Cannot find module '../renative.json'
Require stack:
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/config.js
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/tasks/taskExport.js
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/index.js
- ./node_modules/@rnv/core/lib/modules/index.js
Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/app/.appgyver/app/renative.private.json'
 at Object.openSync (node:fs:561:18)
 at Object.readFileSync (node:fs:445:35)
 at handleCryptoApiKeys (/app/current/buildHooks/dist/index.js:29381:56)

 at Object.dynatraceRegularInvoke [as doInvoke] (/opt/dynatrace/oneagent-paas/agent/bin/1.311.79.20250515-130846/any/nodejs/nodejsagent.js:4087:22)
error: ⨯ ○ export: COMMAND: 
node --max-old-space-size=12000 /app/current/node_modules/next/dist/bin/next build 
FAILED with ERROR: 
> Build failed because of webpack errors
Error: Command failed with exit code 1: node --max-old-space-size=12000 /app/current/node_modules/next/dist/bin/next build
 ⚠ Invalid next.config.js options detected: 
 ⚠ Invalid input at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Expected string, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Expected undefined, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Invalid input at "env.appSwitcherTests"
> Build failed because of webpack errors
Attention: Next.js now collects completely anonymous telemetry regarding usage.
This information is used to shape Next.js' roadmap and prioritize features.
You can learn more, including how to opt-out if you'd not like to participate in this anonymous program, by visiting the following URL:
https://nextjs.org/telemetry
 ▲ Next.js 14.2.26
 at makeError (/app/current/node_modules/execa/lib/error.js:60:11)
 at handlePromise (/app/current/node_modules/execa/index.js:118:26)
 at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:105:5)
 ⚠ Invalid next.config.js options detected: 
 ⚠ Invalid input at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Expected string, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"
> Build failed because of webpack errors
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ⨯ SUMMARY | 6/27/2025, 11:36:36 AM | rnv@1.3.0-feat-old-overrides.0
│ $ npx rnv export -p web -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --packageManager yarn
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Project: app@0.1.0 │
│ warn: You have integration @sba-runtime/integration-builder defined, but it wasn't found in package.json. ERR: Error: Cannot find module '../renative.json'

entry.title: CAP Action Not Triggered in SAP Build Process Automation Workflow Form
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m working with SAP Build Process Automation and running into an issue where a CAP function is not being triggered from within a workflow form.

The action is defined as follows in CAP CDS, Cloud foundry runtime:

function functionQueryLandscapeObjects(
    search: Integer
) returns array of UILandscapeObjects;

In SAP Build Apps, I’ve added the service via the Action Editor and tested it there — everything works as expected. When I provide a valid search parameter (Business Partner ID), I get the correct data back.

However, in the actual workflow form, the function doesn’t seem to be triggered at all. The setup is like this:

  • The user selects a customer from a dropdown.
  • Based on the selected customer, the functionQueryLandscapeObjects action should be triggered to load related landscapes/systems.
  • The search parameter is properly set up to reference the selected Business Partner ID.
  • But: when the landscape dropdown should be populated, nothing happens. No data is shown.
  • Also, the CAP app doesn’t log anything in Cloud Foundry, which suggests the action isn’t called at all.

I’ve double-checked the destination, the bindings, and the input logic, and everything seems fine.

Has anyone experienced something similar?
Do I need to explicitly wire the action to a form event in Build Process Automation? Or is there a limitation with calling parameterized actions from forms?

Any help or examples would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance,

entry.title: Is there any documentation to enable Joule Studio in SAP Build ?
entry.content:
Hi community,

Is there any documentation or steps to enable Joule Studio in SAP Build. Currently I only see below options. Am I missing any subscriptions/entitlements ?
